programmes.show_description: Whiskey and Sticky and All the Rest Can Wait: Ah Sahm and Hong race to free Young Jun. With Mai Ling in prison, Li Yong plans a rescue mission. (S3, ep 5 of 10)
As the documentary returns for a fifth season, officers race to a stabbing outside a pub in Newcastle and there is a raid on a cannabis farm. (S5, ep 1)
Action-packed documentary following rapid response officers, the fastest cops in Britain, as they crack down on crime. Time is of the essence! (S3, ep 4)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S8, ep 1)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S8, ep 2)
Follow the work of the New Zealand Customs agents in their quest to prevent illegal items passing through the country's borders. (S10, ep 5)
Follow the work of the New Zealand Customs agents in their quest to prevent illegal items passing through the country's borders. (S10, ep 6)
Documentary following the men and women who help keep motorists safe on some of the most perilous highways and byways of New Zealand. (S8, ep 9)
Documentary following the men and women who help keep motorists safe on some of the most perilous highways and byways of New Zealand. (S8, ep 10)
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
A sniper attack on the motorway puts police on full alert, and a man tries to run from police on foot but nature interferes with his getaway. (S16, ep 7)
Cameras capture an unbelievable smash as a drunk pushes his speed to the limit and a man thinks it's a good idea to hitch a ride with police. (S16, ep 8)
Officers are stumped by a backpacker carrying photos of his criminal activities. Plus, an American band gets caught in a jam. (S2, ep 6)
Staff confront an American soldier running scared, and a British woman causes confusion with her bag of tricks. (S2, ep 7)
Fly-on-the-wall documentary following the men and women who defend Australia from drug runners, smugglers and terrorists. (S5, ep 3)

CBP questions a pair of real estate entrepreneurs bound for Florida. In Blaine, a Canadian traveller's US investments are cause for concern. (S1, ep 25)
At O'Hare International, a returning American's bananas cause a slip-up in secondary. A mother covers for her kid at Detroit's Ambassador Bridge. (S1, ep 26)
Biosecurity officer Jade makes an expensive witchcraft-related discovery. Also, some suspicious lampshades reach Customs. (S12, ep 1)
Immigration discovers an attempt to establish a dangerous and illegal business in New Zealand. Meanwhile, Customs uncovers a drugs scam. (S12, ep 2)

The Alternate: Odo's mentor arrives on Deep Space Nine intent on resuming his search for Odo's true origin.
Scorched Earth: SG-1 find themselves caught between two civilisations attempting to colonise the same planet. (S4, ep 9)
Friendship One: With communications to the Alpha Quadrant now established, Starfleet sends Voyager on a mission to retrieve a 21st-century probe called Friendship One. (S7, ep 21)
Datalore: Data's android lookalike formulates an evil masterplan that could destroy the Enterprise.
Officers stop a Vietnamese man wearing very unusual jewellery. Another man claims he has nothing to declare but an inspection proves he is lying... a lot! (S15, ep 13)
An alarming X-ray of a package at the mail centre leaves the Wildlife team rushing to get it open before it's too late. A young man is caught out by the drug detector dog. (S15, ep 14)
You Know Your Dragon Best: The crew attempt to control a dangerous forest fire and save a group of environmentalists protesting a new housing development. (S1, ep 13)
Unburdened: The Fly Team investigates when an American police detective on the federal task force is murdered in Paris. (S2, ep 1)
The Andorian Incident: Archer and his crew encounter hostile, paranoid aliens when they visit a Vulcan monastery and discover it has been taken over by the Andorians. (S1, ep 7)
A Soft Heart Won't Do You No Favours: Chao and Lee band together to face an unexpected threat. Meanwhile, the Hop Wei negotiate with the German traders. (S3, ep 6 of 10)
An emergency dash results in a serious collision between the police van and a taxi. Plus, officers race to save a man threatening to take his own life. (S5, ep 2)
Action-packed documentary following rapid response officers, the fastest cops in Britain, as they crack down on crime. Time is of the essence! (S3, ep 5)

Police call on multiple units and the Eagle helicopter as they hunt a stolen Subaru and a cyclist can't quite comprehend why he can't bike along the motorway. (S16, ep 9)
A Kiwi stag night ditches the Aussie brother-in-law to be and a car crash leads to some unusual behaviour on the side of the motorway. (S16, ep 10)
Hidden compartments in a traveller's backpack raise red flags, and the discovery of a deadly weapon leaves mail centre workers stunned. (S2, ep 8)
Officers look for a message in a bottle, while a lack of papers land a group of LA rappers in the doghouse. Plus, a young man receives a wake-up call. (S2, ep 9)

Officers discover a traveller is driving without a license and a US resident returns from the Dominican Republic with an unusual package. (S1, ep 27)
At Detroit Metro Airport, a Japanese traveller must make a difficult decision. An officer discovers something seedy at the mail facility in Chicago. (S1, ep 28)
Customers uncover concealed drugs. Biosecurity New Zealand find some troublesome items in a spiritual healer's luggage. (S12, ep 3)
A woman is caught body packing highly illegal objects. Meanwhile, there's trouble in Biosecurity. (S12, ep 4)

Armageddon Game: Bashir and O'Brien help to rid two alien races of deadly weapons, unaware that their hosts intend to sacrifice them as part of the peace process.
Beneath the Surface: SG-1 awaken in a mine with no recollection of their former lives. Jonah, Therra, Carlin and Tor - as they are now known - have a new set of memories. (S4, ep 10)
Natural Law: A strange energy barrier causes Chakotay and Seven of Nine's shuttle to crash on a planet inhabited by extremely primitive aliens. (S7, ep 22)
Angel One: The Enterprise crew travels to Angel One, a planet run by women, to search for survivors from a Federation freighter disabled by an asteroid collision seven years earlier.
Officers investigate the suspicious luggage of a man returning from China and Vietnam. Drug detector dog Vulcan makes an amazing find. (S15, ep 15)
Border Force zero in on two men trying to avoid luggage checks as a search of their bags reveals why. There's a million-dollar find in the Mail Centre. (S15, ep 16)
Hand-Off: Officer Nolan finds out his identity has been stolen, potentially threatening his position as an officer. (S2, ep 15)
The Overnight: Officers Chen and Bradford meet Ryan Seacrest and the judges when they respond to a call at the American Idol auditions in LA. (S2, ep 16)
Breaking The Ice: Trip learns that T'Pol has been transmitting secret messages to a nearby Vulcan ship, and Archer attempts to save two crewmen from a disintegrating comet. (S1, ep 8)
